name: Fuente del Canto Trail (PR-TE 130)
name_original: Fuente del Canto – circular desde Bronchales (PR-TE 130)
description: Hike the moderate 6.8-mile Fuente del Canto Trail (PR-TE 130) in Sierra de Albarracín, exploring springs and forested paths.
region_intro: Hiking around Bronchales offers exploration of a high-altitude landscape, situated at 1,569 meters in the Sierra de Albarracín. The region is characterized by extensive pine forests, including Scots pine and juniper, providing shaded paths. Numerous natural springs, such as Fuente del Canto and Fuente del Hierro, contribute to the area's water features. Hikers can also encounter unique geological formations like "Rivers of Stones" and dolines, alongside panoramic views from elevated points like Sierra Alta. Best hiking trails around Bronchales The most popular hiking route is Sierra Alta – circular from Bronchales (PR-TE 131), a 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route ascends through pine forests to offer expansive views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Fuente del Canto Trail (PR-TE 130), a moderate 6.8 miles (10.9 km) path. This trail explores several natural springs and forested areas, providing a diverse natural experience. Local hikers also love the Camping Las Corralizas – Sierra Alta Summit loop from Bronchales, a 2.8 miles (4.6 km) trail leading through pine forests towards the Sierra Alta summit, often completed in about 1 hour 19 minutes. Hiking around Bronchales is defined by extensive pine forests, abundant natural springs, and high-altitude viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to moderate ascents. The routes in Bronchales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Bronchales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Bronchales?
A: Bronchales offers over 30 hiking trails, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a diverse range of routes, from easy loops to more challenging ascents through the pine forests.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Bronchales has 9 easy trails perfect for beginners or families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camping Las Corralizas – Sierra Alta Summit loop from Bronchales&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.6 km route that takes about 1 hour 19 minutes to complete, leading through beautiful pine forests.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Bronchales?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Bronchales are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979619171/sierra-alta-circular-from-bronchales-pr-te-131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Alta – circular from Bronchales (PR-TE 131)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 8.9 km loop offering expansive views. Another excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bronchales Town Center – Fuente del Canto loop from Bronchales&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10 km route.

Q: Are there any trails that feature natural springs or water sources?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural springs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981098581/fuente-del-canto-trail-pr-te-130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Canto Trail (PR-TE 130)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.9 km path specifically designed to explore several natural springs and forested areas, providing a diverse natural experience. Fuente del Canto itself is a popular recreational area.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the hiking trails?
A: Bronchales, often called 'The balcony of Spain,' offers stunning panoramic views due to its elevated position. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979619171/sierra-alta-circular-from-bronchales-pr-te-131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Alta – circular from Bronchales (PR-TE 131)&lt;/a&gt; ascend through pine forests to the Sierra Alta summit, providing breathtaking vistas of the Sierra de Albarracín and, on clear days, even Moncayo.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see near the trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portera Refuge and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers great views and a place to rest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3620455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Alta Summit&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive panoramas. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5369830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bronchales Town Center&lt;/a&gt; itself, or the nearby villages of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2530405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orihuela del Tremedal&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5376107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Griegos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Bronchales?
A: Yes, Bronchales is well-equipped for hikers. You'll typically find parking available in or near the town center, which serves as a starting point for many routes. For trails beginning further out, such as those near Camping Las Corralizas, dedicated parking areas are usually provided.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bronchales?
A: The trails in Bronchales are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the extensive pine forests, the abundance of natural springs, and the well-maintained paths that offer a serene and immersive outdoor experience.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Bronchales?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in Bronchales. The extensive pine forests and natural environment provide a great setting for walks with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to follow local regulations regarding waste disposal.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bronchales?
A: The best time to hike in Bronchales is typically during spring, summer, and early autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures for exploring the high-altitude landscapes and enjoying the lush pine forests. Spring brings blooming flora, while autumn showcases beautiful fall colors. Winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate gear due to potential snow and colder conditions.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking routes that pass through Bronchales?
A: Yes, Bronchales is part of significant long-distance routes. It's included in two stages of the historic Camino del Cid (Stage 38: Checa – Bronchales and Stage 39: Bronchales – Albarracín). Additionally, stages 10 and 11 of the GR 10 long-distance path pass through Bronchales, connecting to Monterde de Albarracín and Orihuela del Tremedal.
tips: The Singing Fountain is not only what its name indicates, but you can also find a couple of shelters in very good condition, tables and chairs to spend a pleasant moment in this wonderful place. In short, it is a recreational and picnic area that is worth visiting if you are in the area!

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Fuente del Canto Trail (PR-TE 130)?
faqs.answer: The entire loop typically takes about 3 hours to complete, making it a good option for a half-day outing.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Fuente del Canto Trail (PR-TE 130) is classified as a moderate hike. While it involves some ascent and varied terrain, it's generally manageable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness. Beginners who are prepared for a few hours of walking should find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: Is it a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This is a convenient circular hiking route, meaning it starts and ends in the same location without requiring you to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail officially begins from the Plaza de la Fuente in Bronchales. As it's a circular route starting from the village, you can typically find parking within Bronchales itself.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Fuente del Canto Trail (PR-TE 130)?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. It starts on a road before transitioning to tracks, forested areas, and sections with vehicle tracks. You'll also encounter a 'Río de Piedras' (River of Stones) and clear paths. Sturdy hiking shoes are recommended due to the diverse surfaces.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is notable for its series of natural springs, or 'fuentes,' including Fuente de El Chorrillo, Fuente de El Pilar, Fuente del Tío Pelús, and Fuente del Ojuelo. The main highlight is the Fuente del Canto itself. You'll also pass near the Cruz de Las Almas, a traditional spot where hikers often leave a stone.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Fuente del Canto Trail (PR-TE 130)?
faqs.answer: Given the natural springs and forested sections, spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking this trail. The weather is milder, and the scenery is vibrant. Summer can be pleasant, especially in the shaded forest parts, but always check local temperatures. Winter might bring snow, adding a different challenge and beauty.

faqs.question: Is the Fuente del Canto Trail (PR-TE 130) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this trail aren't detailed, generally, dogs are welcome on trails in natural areas like this, especially if kept on a leash. Always ensure your dog is under control and that you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Fuente del Canto Trail (PR-TE 130)?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Fuente del Canto Trail (PR-TE 130). It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the Fuente del Canto Trail (PR-TE 130) connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other trails. Notably, it covers 100% of the 'Fuente del Canto' trail, and also passes through parts of 'Las Tres Taifas' (43%), 'MontañasVacías' (39%), and 'Sierra Alta' (25%). You'll also find smaller overlaps with routes like 'Sendero Canto 2' and 'Senda a Bronchales'.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or fees for hiking in Sierra de Albarracín?
faqs.answer: The Fuente del Canto Trail (PR-TE 130) is located within the broader Sierra de Albarracín region. While there are no specific permits or fees for this particular trail, it's always good practice to respect general outdoor ethics: stay on marked paths, do not litter, and be mindful of local wildlife and flora. Always check for any temporary local restrictions, especially during fire season.
